justification: B is better calibrated on skills: it correctly separates AI/ML tools (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini API, LangChain, RAG, Prompt Engineering, Vector Stores, MCP) into their own category, preserving all demonstrated tools from the resume without omission, while A drops MCP and the full AI tools grouping into a less organized structure. B also avoids A's slight inflation of framing (e.g., A adds 'infrastructure-as-code best practices' which is not stated in the resume — a minor fabrication). Both capture impact metrics equally well (52% latency reduction, 98% time reduction, 83% accuracy, 5 hrs/week saved, 1,000+ orders). Project classification is equivalent between the two. B's skill organization is more honest and complete, earning it the win on skill_accuracy and conservatism.
